🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-17 14:39 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?Improving, not yet — 1x 🚨 unchanged (INC-8064 H2Bet duplicate bonuses, still static 50+ days, REW-176 parked to mid-Aug) and 3x 🔴 (INC-14177 campeon partner-push ~32h overdue, INC-13917 incident.io never updated despite partner side closed, INC-12179 recurring LLM-agent failure 13+ days with all follow-ups outstanding). Big improvement vs last digest: INC-12246 (Lottomart security P1) confirmed fixed and deployed by Andrea today, dropping from 🚨; INC-12617 closed; INC-10902 (H2Bet nightly) got a fresh owner (Matteo) from Andrea today, dropping from 🔴. Andrea ran an active incident sweep this cycle — several leads reassigned, severities corrected, and closures pushed through live during this run.
Urgent vs smaller1 urgent (🚨), 3 need a nudge (🔴), 13 watch-level (🟡), 7 well-handled (🟢)
Total open (active, excl. triage)24 -9 vs last digest (24 vs 33) -- 3 new, 12 resolved
1
🚨 Escalate now
3
🔴 Needs attention
13
🟡 Watch
7
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14513 — P2 mrbit lf-timeinstance queue — fast ack, resolved within ~45min, clean
  • INC-14503 — P2 Meridianbet-wide processing delay, 12+ alerts merged — well mitigated (Singularity scaled) but root cause still 'Unknown' in the record
  • INC-14521 — P2 2nd CF Threat Watch alert today — correct security escalation, Patrik/Agustín/Stephen already engaged, too fresh to assess further

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • INC-14301 — ⚠ Star Casino NL queue — assigned lead left channel with no closing update; now closed bare, still undocumented (C10/D15)
  • INC-14151 — ⚠ MeridianBet IC ticket — closed bare; partner re-check request was ~19.5h unanswered at last check, no confirmation it was resolved before close
  • INC-13738 — ⚠ Betiro no-unsubs-since-April — closed bare; legal/compliance escalation, retroactive remediation, and 3 newly-flagged brands were all still unaddressed at last check (D12/C10)
  • INC-12754 — Campeon Nexto login — fix long since confirmed working; now formally closed, resolving the earlier missing-closing-note gap
  • INC-9820 — ⚠ RDS OOM all brands — global Performance Schema fix appears to have completed rollout, but the resilience-hardening follow-up's status is unconfirmed at closure (A4)
  • INC-14440 — ⚠ Shuffle dashboards — false-positive cause confirmed, but the ownership-bounce (SRE vs Cloud) was never visibly resolved before close (B5/D12)
  • INC-14474 — ⚠ OneCasino queue (1st of 3 same-day incidents on this queue) — real fix applied (DB index + more pods) but never consolidated with INC-14494 as recommended; closed separately (A4)
  • INC-14494 — ⚠ OneCasino queue (3rd same-day incident, same queue as INC-14474) — closed separately rather than merged, so the recurrence pattern isn't tracked as one problem (A4)
  • INC-14471 — Rhino email-preview WAF block — clean, IPs unblocked, closed with no open breaches
  • INC-14483 — rhino slow-processing — pods restarted, backlog cleared, closed clean
  • INC-13916 — BrazilBet phishing/spoofing — Head of SRE + CTO made an explicit informed close decision (D12), textbook handling, closed clean
  • INC-12617 — ⚠ Fusion API 401s (original occurrence) — closed today with no partner re-chase in 9+ days and no mention of the still-open recurrence pattern (INC-13895) in the closing action
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 1

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 51d· evidence to 09 Jul 09:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

~300 duplicate bonus grants/week continuing after 50+ days; the accepted interim workaround (REW-176) is parked until mid-Aug by Rewards capacity, and the BetConstruct root-cause path has zero traction.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed root cause quickly, but 50+ days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. Per management note, H2Bet accepted the interim workaround (REW-176), opened 9 Jul, but it remains blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug. No change this cycle — 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts, situation fully static, financial leakage continuing unabated.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50+ days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
  • B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction
  • C10/D15: Intercom closed (since 06 Jul) implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), opened Urgent 9 Jul, blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug
Timeline (2)
  • No new Slack or Intercom activity this cycle
  • No new activity this cycle -- ~300 duplicate grants/week continuing, REW-176 still parked until mid-Aug

→ Decide this week: pull REW-176 forward in Rewards' queue, or formally document risk-acceptance of the leakage until mid-Aug; separately escalate the BetConstruct account-manager gap to leadership.

🟡 Watch 13

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 15d· evidence to 17 Jul 13:55 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Per management note: solved as an incident, only the partner cross-check remains open — active work continuing (Giancarlo checked again today), not stalled.

Cloudflare-origin check blocked shortlink/unsubscribe traffic; mitigated same day, fix merged 02 Jul. Per Andrea's management note this is solved as an incident, pending only Estelarbet's cross-check of impacted players. Giancarlo's cross-check today (11:44 UTC) came back inconclusive — logs for the claimed complainant don't clearly show the reported unsubscribe. Status moved Monitoring → Waiting on Partner.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 28d· evidence to 17 Jul 09:55 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Continued thorough, collaborative technical investigation (Matteo/Stephen) into event-ordering reliability — no breach, still resolving a genuine data-modeling question before deciding on a fix.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. Andrea sent Matteo's drafted handover to Integration/PM (Robert Friberg, Lis, Gui Furst) this morning, framing it as needing a permanent dev fix. Robert pushed back that GiG should fix their own event ordering rather than FT building custom logic, but asked SRE/Integration to scope how big a change the timestamp-based (update_epoch) fix would be as a fallback.

Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • update_epoch as ordering key - drafted handover identifies this as a candidate fix for Integration/dev to assess, with an honest feasibility writeup
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul - awaiting Andrea's go-ahead to send
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jul 10:03 CEST Matteo posts a fully-drafted partner message and an internal SRE→Integration/PM handover memo (root cause, options, open decisions), asks Andrea for sign-off
  • 16 Jul 14:58-15:13 CEST Matteo tells partner (Anna) a correction pass for accounts affected since 30 Jun is coming, and that he'll regroup internally with Lis/Integration on the long-term fix -- partner satisfied for now ('sounds great, thanks')
  • 17 Jul 07:58 CEST Andrea sends the full SRE->Integration/PM handover to Robert Friberg, Lis, and Gui Furst -- root cause, options, and the ask, as drafted by Matteo yesterday
  • 17 Jul 10:22 CEST Robert Friberg: prefers pushing GiG to fix event ordering on their end rather than FT building custom logic, but asks Stephen/Curt to scope the size of the update_epoch-based fallback fix and whether FT has done this before elsewhere
  • 17 Jul 10:57-11:55 CEST: Stephen and Matteo continue rigorous joint analysis of GiG update_epoch reliability for reordering assign/unassign events; conclusion reached that update_epoch (not entry_timestamp) is reliable for ordering since opposite-polarity events for the same tag are never within the same second, despite ~30% of events losing sub-second precision

→ Await Stephen/Curt's scoping answer to Robert, then get a final decision on GiG escalation vs. FT-side timestamp fix.

INC-11079 slackP2open 20d· evidence to 15 Jul 10:46 UTC

INC-14503 slackP2open 113m· evidence to 17 Jul 14:40 UTC
Meridianbet Migration and Sendouts Alerts

Fast, competent scaling response containing a wide multi-service alert storm, but the record doesn't yet explain what actually caused the migration/sendout backlog.

Meridianbet-wide delayed message processing across CRM integration, Singularity, kafka-shovel and RabbitMQ, absorbing 12+ merged alerts/incidents. Joachim took lead instantly, scaled up Singularity, and by 15:31 CEST 7 attached alerts had resolved; status moved to Monitoring. Lead handed to Giancarlo at 16:34 CEST as more alerts kept attaching and resolving through 16:40 CEST. Root cause itself is still recorded as 'Unknown' in incident.io despite the mitigation working.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io 'Causes' field still 'Unknown' despite substantial mitigation already applied
Proposed / pending
  • Scale up Singularity - applied, working
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 12:53 UTC alert fires, 8 alerts attached within the hour, Joachim leads
  • 17 Jul 15:03 CEST Joachim: kafka lag on consumer now processing (after scaling Singularity)
  • 17 Jul 15:30 CEST Andrea updates status to Monitoring; 7 attached alerts resolved
  • 17 Jul 16:34 CEST lead handed to Giancarlo; more alerts still merging/resolving through 16:40 CEST

→ Whoever holds the lead (Giancarlo) to add the root cause once identified, not just the mitigation steps.

Per management note: known partner credit issue, FT awaiting their feedback. Andrea is now actively pushing toward a decision point (disable vs. keep waiting) rather than letting it sit indefinitely.

SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Per management note, this is a known credit-related issue with the partner, who is aware; FT is awaiting their feedback. No new activity this cycle.

🟢 Under control 7

7x well-handled: INC-14513 (mrbit, fast clean fix), INC-14476 (Mystake, rigorous root-cause tracing), INC-14346 & INC-14521 (both CF Threat Watch alerts, correct security escalation), INC-14351 (Stanleybet Romania, partner-agreed Monday fix), INC-14423 (log4j CVE, Patrik personally deprioritized with a tracked ticket), INC-12246 (Lottomart security P1, Andrea confirmed fix deployed today, closing imminent)

INC-12246 slackP1open 13d· evidence to 17 Jul 13:36 UTC
Exploitable Image found in Lottomart Privileged Kubernetes Container

The rollout-confirmation gap that drove the previous 🚨 flag is now closed — Andrea has explicitly confirmed the fix deployed and announced closure; only the formal incident.io status transition is outstanding.

Exploitable image (curl CVEs) in Lottomart's privileged Kubernetes container. Fix (Python replacing curl) validated in lower environments; at 15:34 CEST today Andrea announced to the channel that the fix is confirmed deployed via OneClick and Cloud, and that the incident will be closed. incident.io itself has not yet been formally moved to Closed.

Proposed / pending
  • Replace curl with Python in Ambassador, roll out Lottomart then other brands - confirmed deployed 17 Jul
Timeline (3)
  • 06 Jul rollout plan confirmed (PR #20, test Sandy/ai-alpha, then Lottomart, then other brands)
  • 17 Jul 15:34 CEST Andrea: '@channel will be closing this incident — Confirmed through OC and Cloud that the above fix has been deployed'
  • incident.io status still shows 'Investigating' — formal close pending

INC-14513 slackP2open 67m· evidence to 17 Jul 14:36 UTC
mrbit: mrbit.activitymanager.lf-timeinstance — 61 messages

Fast ack despite a paging miss, quick and effective mitigation — no breach.

mrbit activity-manager queue backlog alert. Paging to Joachim expired but Giancarlo acknowledged immediately; Matteo took over as lead and raised max_routines (5 then 10), bringing CPU/queue depth down within ~45 minutes. Alert resolved, status moved to Monitoring.

Proposed / pending
  • Raise max_routines to relieve queue - applied, working
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 13:39 UTC alert fires, paging to Joachim expires but Giancarlo acknowledges immediately
  • 17 Jul 14:21 CEST Matteo becomes lead, raises max_routines to 5 then 10
  • 17 Jul 14:24 CEST attached alert resolved, status → Monitoring
INC-14521 slackP2open 35m· evidence to 17 Jul 14:43 UTC
CF Threat Watch: api-admin-auth-access on ft-crm.com (34.169.78.229)

Correct, fast B6 security escalation with CTO and security team already engaged; too fresh (~30 min old) to assess further.

Second CF Threat Watch alert today (suspicious admin-auth access), same pattern/handling as INC-14346. Giancarlo acknowledged within seconds and correctly routed to the security team (Stephen Riolo as lead, Agustín and Patrik Potocki both in channel); channel made private. Patrik is personally engaged, asking Giancarlo to check Cloudflare security analytics.

Proposed / pending
  • Verify via Cloudflare security analytics - in progress
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 14:11 UTC alert fires, Giancarlo acknowledges immediately, makes channel private
  • 17 Jul 16:16 CEST Stephen/Agustín/Patrik join; Giancarlo hands off to security team
  • 17 Jul 16:41 CEST Andrea sets lead to Stephen Riolo, status → Investigating
  • 17 Jul 16:43 CEST Patrik asks Giancarlo to check Cloudflare security > analytics
INC-14423 slackP3open 15h· evidence to 17 Jul 13:50 UTC
Container using an image vulnerable to CVE-2021-45046

D12 informed decision: Patrik (CTO) explicitly reviewed and deprioritized to next week with a tracked ticket — not a silent close, a deliberate call.

Log4j CVE-2021-45046 found on a staging container. Andrea reassigned lead to Stephen Riolo (security team) at 15:37 CEST; Patrik Potocki personally weighed in, directing that Agustín ack and a Linear ticket be opened so it can be tackled next week — an explicit, informed deprioritization by the CTO himself.

Proposed / pending
  • Upgrade log4j-core - in progress
  • Linear ticket for next-week fix - per Patrik's direction
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 23:30 UTC incident opened, staging-only Log4j CVE
  • 17 Jul 15:37 CEST Andrea reassigns lead Giancarlo → Stephen Riolo (security team)
  • 17 Jul 15:50 CEST Patrik: 'make sure Agustín acks and have a ticket on the linear board... can be tackled next week'
